dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6518 IH: Securing America’s Fuels Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Davids of Kansas (for herself, Mr. Flood, Mr. Carter of Louisiana, and Mr. Mann) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to reinstate the special rate calculation of the clean fuel production credit with respect to sustainable aviation fuel, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Securing America’s Fuels Act or the SAF Act.

section.header: Extension of clean fuel production credit; reinstatement of special rate calculation for sustainable aviation fuel

paragraph.text: Paragraph (3) of section 45Z(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended to read as follows:

paragraph.header: Special rate for sustainable aviation fuel

subparagraph.text: In the case of a transportation fuel which is sustainable aviation fuel, paragraph (2) shall be applied—

clause.text: in the case of fuel produced at a qualified facility described in paragraph (2)(A), by substituting 35 cents for 20 cents, and
in the case of fuel produced at a qualified facility described in paragraph (2)(B), by substituting $1.75 for $1.00.

subparagraph.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term sustainable aviation fuel means liquid fuel, the portion of which is not kerosene, which is sold for use in an aircraft and which—

subclause.text: the Fischer Tropsch provisions of ASTM International Standard D1655, Annex A1, and

clause.text: is not derived from palm fatty acid distillates or petroleum.

paragraph.text: Section 45Z(c)(1) of such Code, as amended by Public Law 119–21, is amended by striking and the $1.00 amount in subsection (a)(2)(B) and inserting , the $1.00 amount in subsection (a)(2)(B), the 35 cent amount in subsection (a)(3)(A)(i), and the $1.75 amount in subsection (a)(3)(A)(ii).

subsection.text: Section 45Z(g) of such Code, as amended by Public Law 119–21, is amended by striking fuel sold after December 31, 2029 and inserting fuel sold after December 31, 2033.
The amendments made by this paragraph shall apply to fuel produced after December 31, 2025.
